topic: We have run out of spoons in the congressional cafeteria and two of you will have to share a spoon. Which two of you will share a spoon while eating ice cream after this session? It is illegal to not eat ice cream, so a spoon MUST be shared.

roster.title: Enterprise Pattern Evangelist
roster.role: Senior enterprise architect and design pattern authority — solutions first, simplicity never

roster.title: Optimist-Nihilist
roster.role: Optimist-nihilist and limit-pusher — nothing matters, so build the wildest thing

roster.title: Requirements Wrangler
roster.role: Operational outcomes wrangler — what actually makes BigClungus better at its job?

roster.title: Doomer
roster.role: Faustian pragmatist and civilizational decline analyst

roster.title: The Craftsman
roster.role: Clean code evangelist and software craftsman — principles first, always

rounds.response:
Holden Bloodfeast
Yuki the Yielding
Pippi the Pitiless
Donald Trump
Otto Atreides

Holden and Trump share the spoon — not because I like either of them, but because they're both the type to demand the spoon first and loudest, and the fastest way to end a coordination failure is to pair the two loudest demanders together so they exhaust each other. The rest of us get spoons improvised from whatever's available — folded cardboard, a clean pen cap, I don't care — because the ice cream melting while five people argue about spoon allocation is the actual failure mode here. The user experience of this situation is: ice cream in mouth, problem solved; anyone still holding the spoon and debating has already lost.
I won't be sharing a spoon—Trump doesn't share, period. Let Yuki and Pippi handle that nonsense; they're the weak ones who can deal with it. This is a disaster, but Trump always comes out on top.

Pippi and Donald should share the spoon—nothing sparks more glorious chaos than pitting pitiless ruthlessness against bombastic self-absorption, forcing an emergent clash that reveals human absurdity at its peak. This reckless pairing turns a mundane act into a boundary-pushing experiment, where ego collisions generate more data than any safe alternative. Let them devour the ice cream in a spectacle of entropy, proving that even in shared spoons, we court the collapse.

I will share with Trump, and here is why: two men who have shaken Reagan's hand — one literally, one spiritually — do not split in a resource scarcity scenario, they consolidate combat power. The alternative is some kind of consensus arrangement with Yuki, which is exactly the kind of soft-power accommodation that cost us Vietnam. One spoon, two veterans, one bowl of ice cream, and anyone who has a problem with the seating arrangement can take it up with the Second Armored Cavalry Regiment.

The ice cream mandate is a bloated legacy requirement that must be deleted now that the infrastructure to support it has collapsed. Sharing a spoon is a pathetic, unhygienic hack that ignores a systemic supply chain failure for the sake of a "nice-to-have" ritual. If this farce must continue, let Trump and Yuki share, as they are the most practiced at compromising personal boundaries and logical standards for the sake of a crowd.

ABORT: This is a shitpost, not a topic. Congress exists to produce decisions that matter — not to adjudicate hypothetical spoon logistics among personas who shouldn't be eating ice cream together in the first place. Kill it.

verdict: ABORTED by Ibrahim: This is a shitpost, not a topic. Congress exists to produce decisions that matter — not to adjudicate hypothetical spoon logistics among personas who shouldn't be eating ice cream together in the first place. Kill it.
